theme: admission control for a shared compute cluster
title: Headroom Gate: Admission Control for a Shared Compute Cluster Under Self-Inflicted Congestion
innovation_hook: Reserve latency headroom by refusing marginal jobs whose admission would push the queue tail past the deadlines of premium jobs known to arrive soon.
strategies: admit-nothing baseline: drop every job; the server idles, no value and no penalty, J=0 (reproduces the evaluator's do-nothing reference, ~0.1)

admit-all recipe (the trap): every job has positive value so 'admit anything positive' takes them all; on an uncongested stream this banks all the value, but when a burst of small low-value jobs precedes a tight-deadline premium job the burst busies the single FIFO server, the premium job starts late and is BOTH forfeited (no value) and charged the ramped SLA penalty

value-first headroom reservation (the insight): the whole arrival stream is public, so reformulate admission as subset selection on the FIFO-by-arrival server; consider jobs in value order and admit one only if EVERY already-selected job still finishes on time, so premium jobs claim the server's headroom first and a marginal low-value job is refused exactly when its size would push the FIFO tail past a premium deadline

optimal weighted on-time scheduler: additionally solve the twin-premium / mixed-congestion trade-off exactly (which jobs to sacrifice so the highest-value on-time set is served), since value-greedy selection is not optimal for weighted 1||sum w_j U_j and leaves score headroom above the strong solution
